title: Empirical Determination of the Pion Mass Distribution
author_string: Yin-Zhen Xu, Khépani Raya, Zhu-Fang Cui, Craig D. Roberts, J. Rodríguez-Quintero
abstract: Existing pion+nucleus Drell-Yan and electron+pion scattering data are used to develop ensembles of model-independent representations of the pion generalised parton distribution (GPD). Therewith, one arrives at a data-driven prediction for the pion mass distribution form factor, $θ_2$. Compared with the pion elastic electromagnetic form factor, $θ_2$ is harder: the ratio of the radii derived from these two form factors is $r_π^{θ_2}/r_π= 0.79(3)$. Our data-driven predictions for the pion GPD, related form factors and distributions should serve as valuable constraints on theories of pion structure.
comment: 9 pages, 6 figures, 1 table. Version accepted for publication as an Express Letter in Chin. Phys. Lett
